title: Medicine's Biggest Hormone Error: Progesterone vs. Progestin
description: The Women's Health Initiative scared a generation of women off hormone therapy — but the drug at the center of that study wasn't progesterone. It was a synthetic substitute with a different molecular structure, different receptor profile, and different risks. This piece investigates how the conflation of 'progesterone' and 'progestin' became medicine's most consequential terminology error, what the evidence actually shows about each, and why the distinction still hasn't reached most prescribing physicians.
